name: Calvine Incident
description: The Calvine Incident refers to a purported UFO sighting and photograph taken near Calvine, Scotland, in August 1990. Two men, a chef and a hotel worker, claimed to have witnessed a large, diamond-shaped object hovering silently in the sky, accompanied by military fighter jets. They took several photographs, one of which became known as the 'best UFO picture ever taken.' The photos were reportedly handed over to the Scottish Daily Record newspaper, who then passed them on to the Ministry of Defence (MoD). The MoD allegedly suppressed the images, and the original photograph disappeared. Proponents believe this constitutes evidence of a government cover-up regarding UFO activity. The incident gained renewed attention in 2022 when the original photograph was released after being kept secret for over 30 years. Mainstream perspectives generally consider the Calvine Incident unresolved. Explanations range from misidentification of conventional aircraft or meteorological phenomena, to elaborate hoaxes. Skeptics point to the lack of independent corroboration and the potential for photographic manipulation, even with 1990s technology. The MoD's handling of the case, including the initial suppression of the photo, has fueled conspiracy theories, while official sources claim it was handled according to standard procedures for investigating potential threats to national security.
subtitle: Scotland's 'best UFO picture' mystery.

claims.claim: A large, diamond-shaped UFO was sighted and photographed near Calvine in 1990.
claims.evidence: Eyewitness accounts and the Calvine photograph.
claims.criticism: Lack of independent corroboration, potential for misidentification or hoax.

claims.claim: Military fighter jets were present near the UFO, suggesting military involvement or monitoring.
claims.evidence: Eyewitness accounts describing the presence of military aircraft.
claims.criticism: Possible misidentification of aircraft, or coincidental presence. The released photo isn't entirely clear on this point.

claims.claim: The Ministry of Defence (MoD) suppressed the photograph and covered up the incident.
claims.evidence: The MoD's initial withholding of the photograph and the subsequent disappearance of the original negative.
claims.criticism: The MoD claims the case was handled according to standard procedures and that the photograph was analyzed for national security implications. The delay in release aligns with standard declassification procedures.

keyFigures.name: Kevin Russell
keyFigures.role: One of the original witnesses who took the photograph.

keyFigures.name: Craig Lindsay
keyFigures.role: Former MoD press officer who handled the case.

keyFigures.name: David Clarke
keyFigures.role: UFO historian and journalist who investigated and publicized the incident, eventually securing release of the image.

mythologyConnections: Modern interpretations of fairies/sprites as extraterrestrials

timeline.event: Reported sighting of the UFO near Calvine, Scotland.
The photograph is handed over to the Scottish Daily Record and then to the MoD.
The original photograph is released by Dr. David Clarke after being kept secret for over 30 years.
